audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with apocalyptic and prophetic symbolism; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for apocalypse/unveiling, seven assemblies, Alpha/Omega, Son-of-Human vision, fear-not commission.

verses.restored: Unveiling of Jesus Messiah, which the Divine gave him to show his servants what must happen in swiftness, and he signified it, sending through his messenger to his servant John,
who witnessed the word of the Divine and the witness of Jesus Messiah, as many things as he saw.
Blessed is the one reading and those hearing the words of the prophecy and keeping the things written in it, for the season is near.
John to the seven assemblies in Asia: grace to you and peace from the One who is and who was and who is coming, and from the seven Spirits before his throne,
and from Jesus Messiah, the trustworthy witness, firstborn of the dead, and ruler of kings of earth; to the One loving us and releasing us from our sins/misalignments in his blood/life,
and making us a reign, priests to his Divine and Father, to him be glory and might into the ages. Amen.
Look, he comes with the clouds, and every eye will see him, even those who pierced him, and all tribes of earth will mourn over him. Yes, amen.
I am the Alpha and the Omega, says Lord Divine, the One who is and who was and who is coming, the Almighty.
I, John, your sibling and partner in the pressure and reign and endurance in Jesus, came to be on the island called Patmos because of the word of the Divine and witness of Jesus.
I came to be in Spirit on the Lord's day, and I heard behind me a great voice like trumpet,
saying: What you see, write into a scroll and send to the seven assemblies: Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
And I turned to see the voice speaking with me, and turning I saw seven golden lampstands,
and in midst of the lampstands one like son of human, clothed with a robe reaching to feet and girded at the chest with golden sash.
His head and hairs were white like wool, like snow, and his eyes like flame of fire,
and his feet like burnished bronze refined in furnace, and his voice like sound of many waters.
And having in his right hand seven stars, and from his mouth a sharp two-edged sword going out, and his face like sun shining in its power.
And when I saw him, I fell at his feet as dead; and he placed his right hand on me, saying: Do not fear. I am first and last,
and the Living One; and I became dead, and look, I am living into the ages of ages, and I have keys of Death and Hades.
Therefore write what you saw, and what is, and what is about to happen after these things.
The mystery of the seven stars you saw upon my right hand and the seven golden lampstands: the seven stars are messengers of the seven assemblies, and the seven lampstands are seven assemblies.

translation_notes: Apokalypsis is unveiling, not disaster genre.
Sword from mouth is symbolic word/judgment imagery, not literal violence.
Revelation opens as witness under empire-pressure, not a codebook for modern target-mapping.
safety_note: Revelation must be read as symbolic apocalyptic resistance literature centered on the Lamb, not as fearmongering, hate, or violent fantasy.
